LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFFS SANTIAGO ROBLETO, AGUSTIN ROBLETO, ALEJANDRO ROBLETO AND MARGARITA LOPEZ'S MOTION FOR REFERENCE TO PRESIDING JUDGE FOR DETERMINATION OF PRESIDING JUDGE'S DESIGNATION OF DESIRED DEPARTMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF CITES NO BASIS FOR HIS MOTION FOR REFERENCE TO THE PRESIDING JUDGE. LOCAL RULE 2.0 IS INAPPOSITE AND DOES NOT SUPPORT THE MOTION. TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO CONSOLIDATE THIS CIVIL ACTION WITH THE PENDING PROBATE MATTER, THE REQUEST IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT CONSOLIDATION IS APPROPRIATE. TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS A RULING DECIDING WHETHER THIS DEPARTMENT HAS JURISDICTION TO HEAR THIS CASE, THE REQUEST IS ALSO DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN WHETHER THIS CASE INVOLVES ISSUES WHICH ARE WITHIN THE EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION OF THE PROBATE COURT OR ISSUES THAT ARE MERELY WITHIN THE CONCURRENT JURISDICTION OF THE PROBATE COURT AND THIS DEPARTMENT. MOREOVER, EVEN IF THIS CASE DOES INVOLVE ISSUES WITHIN THE EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION OF THE PROBATE COURT THAT DOES NOT MEAN THAT THIS COURT LACKS SUBJECT MATTER JURISDICTION TO HEAR THIS CASE. (SEE HARNEDY V. WHITTY (2003) 110 CAL.APP.4TH 1333, 1344-1345; IN RE MICHAEL R. (2006) 137 CAL.APP.4TH 126, 146; ESTATE OF BOWLES (2008) 169 CAL.APP.4TH 684, 695.) IF DEFENDANT FAILS TO OBJECT TO THIS DEPARTMENT'S JURISDICTION AND INSTEAD FULLY PARTICIPATES IN THESE PROCEEDINGS, DEFENDANT IS BARRED BY PRINCIPLES OF WAIVER AND ESTOPPEL FROM RAISING THE ISSUE ON APPEAL. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JULIA VARGAS' RE-NOTICED MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER & PROPER RESPONSES TO VARGAS' FIRST SET OF REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS - THE COURT ADOPTS IN FULL THE RECOMMENDATION OF PRO TEM JUDGE RENNE AS FOLLOWS: DENIED AS UNTIMELY NOTICED. CCP 2033.290(C) REQUIRES THAT A NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL MUST BE FILED WITHIN 45 DAYS AFTER RECEIPT OF THE ALLEGEDLY IMPROPER RESPONSE. IT APPEARS FROM THE PAPERS THAT DEFENDANT JULIA VARGAS DID FILE AN INITIAL NOTICE OF HER INTENT TO FILE A MOTION TO COMPEL WITHIN THE 45 DAYS SPECIFIED IN THE STATUTE; HOWEVER, WHEN IT WAS POINTED OUT BY PLAINTIFFS THAT THE PAPERS FILED WITH THE INITIAL MOTION DID NOT INCLUDE THE REQUIRED SEPARATE STATEMENT, DEFENDANT, RATHER THAN PROCEEDING WITH THE PROPERLY NOTICED MOTION, CHOSE TO WITHDRAW THE PENDING MOTION AND FILED WHAT IT DESCRIBED AS A "RE-NOTICE OF MOTION" ON OCTOBER 2, 2012. THIS NOTICE OF MOTION WAS OUTSIDE THE PERIOD PROVIDED FOR BY CCP 2033.290(C) AND A PARTY'S UNILATERAL ACTION IN EXTENDING THE PERIOD OF TIME IS INEFFECTIVE. THE COURT WILL IMPOSE NO MONETARY SANCTIONS AS PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSES TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WERE CLEARLY IMPROPER AND, HAD THIS MOTION BEEN TIMELY FILED THE COURT WOULD HAVE ORDERED PLAINTIFFS TO EITHER ADMIT OR DENY THE REQUESTS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

Plaintiffs Santiago Robleto, Agustin Robleto, Alejandro Robleto And Margarita Lopez'S Motion For Reference To Presiding Judge For Determination Of Presiding Judge'S Designation Of Desired Department
Off Calendar Mar-08-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. NICK DAMER, TEL. NO. (650) 726-5588 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PETER W. CLERIDES, TEL. NO. 732-1744 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Santiago Robleto, Agustin Robleto, Alejandro Robleto And Margarita Lopez's Motion For Reference To Presiding Judge For Determination Of Presiding Judge's Designation Of Desired Department is Denied. Plaintiff cites no basis for his motion for reference to the Presiding Judge. Local Rule 2.0 is inapposite and does not support the motion. To the extent that Plaintiff seeks to consolidate this civil action with the pending probate matter, the request is denied. Plaintiff has not shown that consolidation is appropriate. To the extent that Plaintiff seeks a ruling deciding whether this department has jurisdiction to hear this case, the request is also denied. Plaintiff has not shown whether this case involves issues which are within the exclusive jurisdiction of the Probate Court or issues that are merely within the concurrent jurisdiction of the Probate Court and this department. Moreover, even if this case does involve issues within the exclusive jurisdiction of the Probate Court that does not mean that this Court lacks subject matter jurisdiction to hear this case. (See Harnedy v. Whitty (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1333, 1344-1345; In re Michael R. (2006) 137 Cal.App.4th 126, 146; Estate of Bowles (2008) 169 Cal.App.4th 684, 695.) If Defendant fails to object to this department's jurisdiction and instead fully participates in these proceedings, Defendant is barred by principles of waiver and estoppel from raising the issue on appeal. (Harnedy v. Whitty, supra, 110 Cal.App.4th at p. 1345; In re Michael R., supra, 137 Cal.App.4th at p. 146.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

Re-Notice Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further & Proper Responses To Vargas'S First Set Of Req.'S For Admissions; Memo Of P & A
Off Calendar Nov-21-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT JULIA VARGAS' Re-Noticed Motion To Compel Further & Proper Responses To Vargas' First Set Of Requests For Admissions - The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Renne as follows: Denied as untimely noticed. CCP 2033.290(c) requires that a notice of motion to compel must be filed within 45 days after receipt of the allegedly improper response. It appears from the papers that Defendant Julia Vargas did file an initial notice of her intent to file a motion to compel within the 45 days specified in the statute; however, when it was pointed out by Plaintiffs that the papers filed with the initial motion did not include the required separate statement, Defendant, rather than proceeding with the properly noticed motion, chose to withdraw the pending motion and filed what it described as a "Re-Notice of Motion" on October 2, 2012. This notice of motion was outside the period provided for by CCP 2033.290(c) and a party's unilateral action in extending the period of time is ineffective. The court will impose no monetary sanctions as Plaintiffs' responses to the Requests for Admission were clearly improper and, had this motion been timely filed the court would have ordered Plaintiffs to either admit or deny the requests. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = 302/EG

Off Calendar Nov-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Peter W. Clerides, Esq., Law Office, 415-732-1744, for Defendant and moving party: Julia Vargas
Nicholas R. Damer, Esq., Law Office, 650-726-5588, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: DEFENDANT JULIA VARGAS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained with 15 days leave to amend on the issue of standing. Santiago Robleto Jr. may pursue his claims in his individual capacity, but lacks standing to bring the causes of action in the First Amended Complaint on behalf of the Estate of Dido Caldera. See Probate Code 9820. Demurrer is overruled on all other grounds. Plaintiff has sufficiently stated causes of action for fraud, breach of fiduciary duty, and declaratory relief, and imposition of a constructive trust. Filing an amended complaint while a demurrer is pending is not a judicial admission that the initial complaint was insufficient. Plaintiffs' allegations in the First Amended Complaint relate back to the original complaint because they arise out of the same set of facts. It is not clear from the face of First Amended Complaint or in matters that can be judicially noticed that Plaintiffs claims are time-barred. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice of Exhibit G (2007 letter) is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
